seed_problem: Build a connected relay network where no node has degree > 3, minimizing total cable length; checker validates connectivity+degree and scores baseline/cost.
mutation: input+output: keep the min-cost connectivity goal but relax it from spanning ALL nodes to spanning only a designated hut subset over an arbitrary weighted graph of candidate trenches, with non-hut sites usable as optional relay junctions. This turns the polynomial minimum spanning tree into the NP-hard Steiner tree in graphs.
title: Aurora Station Trench Network: Connecting the Polar Research Base
strategies: backbone baseline: dig the guaranteed backbone path (trenches 1..N-1); always feasible, matches the checker baseline, ratio ~0.1
shortest-path star: run one Dijkstra from the first hut and dig the union of shortest-path trenches to every other hut
Takahashi-Matsuyama shortest-path heuristic: grow the tree from one hut, repeatedly attach the nearest still-disconnected hut via a shortest path, then prune non-hut leaf trenches
metric-closure MST (Kou-Markowsky-Berman) plus local rerouting / leaf pruning for a tighter Steiner tree
